title: DPmixGPD
text:
Bayesian semiparametric modeling for heavy-tailed outcomes using Dirichlet-process mixtures with optional GPD tails, with conditional and causal extensions.
Getting Started Decision Guide Reference (pkgdown)

Explore the curated documentation in Docs or jump to FAQ.

title: DPmixGPD
text:
Latest update

Documentation is now split cleanly: Quarto provides the curated front door, while pkgdown remains the authoritative vanilla API reference for developers.
Read the update

title: DPmixGPD
text:
Where to start


New users
If this is your first time here: 1. Start with the minimal workflow
2. Use the Decision Guide to pick a backend + kernel
3. Run your first Cookbook example
Getting Started Decision Guide


Applied users
If you already know what you want to model: - Choose a kernel family and tail strategy - Jump to a workflow template - Use pkgdown when you need exact arguments / return types
Kernels Cookbook pkgdown


Developers & contributors
If you want internals, build notes, or to contribute: - Developer workflow and repo structure - Coverage + diagnostics pages - Issues/Discussions for feedback
Developers Coverage

title: DPmixGPD
text:
Choose your path


New users
Start with a minimal workflow, then use the Decision Guide to select a backend + kernel.
Getting Started Decision Guide


Applied users
Go straight to the Docs hub, pick kernels, and use the Cookbook for templates. For exact arguments, use pkgdown.
